title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice.
follow_up_hints: Can you do it in O(n) time with a hash set?

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s.
constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: What if the inputs contain Unicode characters?

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target.
follow_up_hints: Can you do it in one pass with a hash map?

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints: strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Can you sort each string as a canonical key?

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements.
constraints: k is in range [1, # unique elements]
follow_up_hints: Can you solve it in O(n log n) or better with bucket sort?

title: Encode and Decode Strings
description: Design an algorithm to encode a list of strings to a single string and decode it back.
follow_up_hints: Use length-prefixed encoding: '4#word'.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] equals the product of all elements except nums[i]. Solve without division in O(n).
follow_up_hints: Use prefix products then suffix products.

title: Valid Sudoku
description: Determine if a 9x9 Sudoku board is valid. Each row, column, and 3×3 box must contain digits 1-9 without repetition.
follow_up_hints: Use three sets per row, column, and 3×3 box.

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Given an unsorted array of integers nums, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. Solve in O(n).
follow_up_hints: Use a hash set; only start counting from sequence beginnings.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters to lowercase and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward. Return true if s is a palindrome.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

constraints: s consists only of printable ASCII characters
follow_up_hints: Use two pointers from both ends, skipping non-alphanumeric characters.

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
description: Given a 1-indexed sorted array, find two numbers that add up to target. Return their 1-based indices.
follow_up_hints: Use two pointers from both ends.

title: 3Sum
description: Given an integer array nums, return all triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i, j, k are distinct and their sum is zero. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.
follow_up_hints: Sort first; for each element use two pointers for the remaining pair.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given n vertical lines at positions 1..n with heights height[i], find two lines that form the container holding the most water.
follow_up_hints: Move the shorter pointer inward — why is this always safe?

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.
follow_up_hints: Water at position i = min(max_left, max_right) - height[i]. Can you do it with two pointers?

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a stock on day i, return the maximum profit from one buy-sell transaction.
follow_up_hints: Track the running minimum and compute profit at each step.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces
follow_up_hints: Sliding window with a set; shrink from the left when a duplicate is found.

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: Given a string s and an integer k, return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter after replacing at most k characters.
constraints: s consists of only uppercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Window is valid if (window_size - max_freq) <= k.

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1.
constraints: s1 and s2 consist of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Sliding window of size len(s1) comparing character frequency maps.

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given strings s and t, return the minimum window substring of s that contains all characters of t.
constraints: s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Expand right pointer until window is valid, then shrink left pointer.

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an array nums and integer k, return the maximum values in each sliding window of size k.
follow_up_hints: Use a deque to maintain candidates for the maximum in decreasing order.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing '(', ')', '{', '}', '[', ']', determine if the input string is valid.
follow_up_hints: Push open brackets; on close, check top of stack matches.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in O(1).
constraints: pop, top, getMin called on non-empty stack
follow_up_hints: Maintain a parallel stack that tracks minimums.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation.
constraints: tokens[i] is either an operator (+,-,*,/) or an integer
follow_up_hints: Push numbers; when you see an operator, pop two and push the result.

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
follow_up_hints: Backtrack: add '(' if open < n, add ')' if close < open.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of integers temperatures, return an array answer where answer[i] is the number of days until a warmer temperature.
follow_up_hints: Monotonic decreasing stack of indices.

title: Car Fleet
description: n cars go to the same destination on a one-lane road. Return the number of car fleets that arrive.
follow_up_hints: Sort by position descending; if a car arrives no later than the one in front, they merge.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar heights, find the largest rectangle area.
follow_up_hints: Monotonic stack: track (index, height) pairs representing the start of each bar's extent.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target value, return the index of target, or -1 if not found.
constraints: All integers in nums are unique
nums is sorted in ascending order
follow_up_hints: Classic binary search with lo/hi pointers.

title: Search a 2D Matrix
description: Given an m x n matrix where each row is sorted and the first integer of each row is greater than the last of the previous row, return true if target is in the matrix.
follow_up_hints: Treat the matrix as a 1D sorted array: index i maps to row i//n, col i%n.

title: Koko Eating Bananas
description: Koko can eat k bananas per hour. Given piles and h hours, return the minimum k such that she can finish all bananas.
follow_up_hints: Binary search on k in range [1, max(piles)].

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a sorted array rotated between 1 and n times, find the minimum element in O(log n).
follow_up_hints: If mid > right, the minimum is in the right half; otherwise it's in the left half.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a rotated sorted array of unique integers, return the index of target or -1.
follow_up_hints: Determine which half is sorted, then decide which half must contain target.

title: Time Based Key-Value Store
description: Design a time-based key-value store. set(key, value, timestamp) stores the value. get(key, timestamp) returns the value with the largest timestamp <= given timestamp.
follow_up_hints: Store list of (timestamp, value) per key; binary search on get.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2, return the median of the two sorted arrays. Solve in O(log(m+n)).
follow_up_hints: Binary search on the partition of the smaller array.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in [0, 5000]
follow_up_hints: Can you do it iteratively and recursively?

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return the merged sorted list.
constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in [0, 50]
follow_up_hints: Use a dummy head node to simplify edge cases.

title: Reorder List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list L0→L1→…→Ln, reorder it to L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1→…
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 5*10^4]
follow_up_hints: Find the middle, reverse the second half, merge the two halves.

title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.
constraints: The number of nodes is sz
follow_up_hints: Use two pointers offset by n; when the fast pointer reaches the end, slow is at the target.

title: Copy List with Random Pointer
description: Construct a deep copy of a linked list where each node has a next and random pointer.
constraints: Node.random is null or points to a node in the list
follow_up_hints: Use a hash map from original node to its copy.

title: Add Two Numbers
description: Given two non-empty linked lists representing non-negative integers stored in reverse order, add them and return the sum as a linked list.
constraints: The number of nodes in each list is in [1, 100]
No leading zeros except the number 0
follow_up_hints: Simulate addition digit by digit with a carry variable.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given the head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]
pos is -1 or a valid index
follow_up_hints: Floyd's cycle detection: slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2 steps.

title: Find the Duplicate Number
description: Given an array nums of n+1 integers where each integer is in [1, n], find the duplicate. Use O(1) extra space without modifying the array.
follow_up_hints: Treat array as a linked list with cycle (Floyd's algorithm).

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure for a Least Recently Used cache with get and put in O(1).
constraints: At most 2*10^5 calls to get and put
follow_up_hints: Use a doubly linked list + hash map. Sentinel head/tail nodes simplify edge cases.

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Merge k sorted linked lists and return the merged sorted list.
follow_up_hints: Use a min-heap with (val, index, node) tuples to always extract the smallest head.

title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
description: Given the head of a linked list, reverse the nodes of the list k at a time and return the modified list.
constraints: The number of nodes is n
follow_up_hints: Count k nodes ahead; if fewer remain, stop. Reverse the group, reconnect to next.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree and return its root.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 100]
follow_up_hints: Swap left and right children recursively.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]
follow_up_hints: DFS: depth = 1 + max(left_depth, right_depth).

title: Diameter of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the length of the diameter (longest path between any two nodes).
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 10^4]
follow_up_hints: At each node, diameter candidate = left_depth + right_depth. Track global max.

title: Balanced Binary Tree
description: Given a binary tree, determine if it is height-balanced (depth of two subtrees differs by at most 1 for every node).
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 5000]
follow_up_hints: Return -1 from DFS to signal imbalance; otherwise return height.

title: Same Tree
description: Given the roots of two binary trees p and q, check whether they are the same.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 100]
follow_up_hints: Recursive: p.val == q.val and same(p.left, q.left) and same(p.right, q.right).

title: Subtree of Another Tree
description: Given the roots of two binary trees root and subRoot, return true if there is a subtree of root with the same structure and node values as subRoot.
constraints: The number of nodes in root is in [1, 2000]
The number of nodes in subRoot is in [1, 1000]
follow_up_hints: Use isSameTree as a helper; recurse on left and right subtrees.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
description: Given a BST and two nodes p and q, find their lowest common ancestor.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [2, 10^5]
follow_up_hints: If both values are less than root, go left; if both greater, go right; else root is LCA.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values (i.e., left to right, level by level).
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 2000]
follow_up_hints: BFS with a queue; use the queue length to process one level at a time.

title: Binary Tree Right Side View
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the values of nodes you can see when looking from the right side.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 100]
follow_up_hints: BFS level order; take the last element of each level.

title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
description: Given a binary tree root, a node X is good if there is no node with value greater than X.val on the path from root to X. Return the number of good nodes.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 10^5]
follow_up_hints: DFS passing the running maximum; a node is good if its value >= max_so_far.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 10^4]
follow_up_hints: DFS with (min_val, max_val) bounds; each node must satisfy min < val < max.

title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
description: Given the root of a BST and an integer k, return the kth smallest value.
constraints: The number of nodes is n
follow_up_hints: In-order traversal of BST yields sorted order; count to k.

title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
description: Given integer arrays preorder and inorder traversals, construct and return the binary tree.
constraints: preorder and inorder consist of unique values
follow_up_hints: Root is preorder[0]; its position in inorder splits left and right subtrees.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
description: A path in a binary tree is a sequence of nodes with edges between adjacent nodes. Given root, return the maximum path sum.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 3*10^4]
follow_up_hints: At each node, the gain = node.val + max(0, left_gain) + max(0, right_gain). Track global max.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize it back.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]
follow_up_hints: Use BFS or preorder DFS with 'null' markers for missing nodes.

title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Implement a trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods.
constraints: word and prefix consist of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Each TrieNode has a children dict and an is_end flag.

title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
description: Design a data structure with addWord(word) and search(word), where search supports '.' wildcard matching any single letter.
constraints: word in addWord consists of lowercase letters
word in search consists of lowercase letters or '.'
follow_up_hints: Extend Trie: at a '.' node, recurse into all children.

title: Word Search II
description: Given an m x n board of characters and a list of words, return all words found in the board.
follow_up_hints: Build a Trie from words, then DFS on the board following Trie nodes.

title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
description: Design a class to find the kth largest element in a stream.
follow_up_hints: Maintain a min-heap of size k; the top is the kth largest.

title: Last Stone Weight
description: Stones are smashed pairwise by weight; if equal both destroyed, else difference survives. Return last stone weight or 0.
follow_up_hints: Use a max-heap (negate values for Python's min-heap).

title: K Closest Points to Origin
description: Given an array of points, return the k closest points to the origin (0, 0).
follow_up_hints: Use a max-heap of size k based on squared distance.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element.
follow_up_hints: Use a min-heap of size k; or quickselect for O(n) average.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given tasks and a cooldown n between identical tasks, return the minimum number of intervals needed.
follow_up_hints: Use a max-heap + queue with (count, available_time) to simulate the scheduler.

title: Design Twitter
description: Design a simplified Twitter: postTweet, getNewsFeed (top 10 recent tweets from followed users), follow, unfollow.
follow_up_hints: Use a global tweet counter. getNewsFeed merges up to 10 recent tweets using a heap.

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Design a data structure that supports adding integers and finding the median in O(log n) add and O(1) find.
constraints: At most 5*10^4 calls to addNum and findMedian
findMedian called after at least one addNum
follow_up_hints: Use a max-heap for the lower half and a min-heap for the upper half.

title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set).
constraints: All elements of nums are unique
follow_up_hints: Backtrack: for each element, either include or skip it.

title: Combination Sum
description: Given an array of distinct candidates and a target, return all unique combinations that sum to target. Each number may be used unlimited times.
constraints: All elements of candidates are distinct
follow_up_hints: Backtrack with index to avoid re-using earlier elements.

title: Permutations
description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations.
constraints: All integers of nums are unique
follow_up_hints: Backtrack by swapping elements with the current position.

title: Subsets II
description: Given an integer array nums that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets. The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets.
follow_up_hints: Sort first; skip duplicate elements at the same recursion level.

title: Combination Sum II
description: Given a collection candidates (may have duplicates) and target, find all unique combinations summing to target. Each number may only be used once.
follow_up_hints: Sort first; at each level, skip duplicates after the first choice.

title: Word Search
description: Given an m x n grid and a word, return true if the word exists in the grid using adjacent cells (no reuse).
follow_up_hints: DFS from each cell; mark visited in-place and restore after backtracking.

title: Palindrome Partitioning
description: Given a string s, partition s such that every substring is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitioning.
constraints: s consists of only lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Backtrack: try each prefix, if palindrome recurse on the rest.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
description: Given a string containing digits 2-9, return all possible letter combinations the number could represent.
constraints: digits[i] is a digit in the range ['2','9']
follow_up_hints: Map each digit to its letters; backtrack through all combinations.

title: N-Queens
description: Place n queens on an n×n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Return all distinct solutions.
follow_up_hints: Backtrack row by row; track used columns, diagonals (/), and anti-diagonals (\).

title: Number of Islands
description: Given an m x n binary grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands.
follow_up_hints: DFS/BFS from each unvisited '1', marking visited cells.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 100]
follow_up_hints: BFS/DFS with a hash map from original node to its clone.

title: Max Area of Island
description: Given a binary matrix grid, return the maximum area of an island (0 if none).
follow_up_hints: DFS from each unvisited 1; count cells, track max.

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
description: Return all cells where water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic ocean (water flows to equal or lower neighbors).
follow_up_hints: BFS/DFS in reverse from Pacific edges and Atlantic edges; intersect the reachable sets.

title: Surrounded Regions
description: Given an m x n matrix of 'X' and 'O', capture all 'O' regions not connected to the border.
follow_up_hints: DFS from border 'O's, marking them safe; then flip all unmarked 'O's.

title: Rotting Oranges
description: Grid cells are 0 (empty), 1 (fresh orange), or 2 (rotten). Each minute, rotten oranges infect adjacent fresh ones. Return minutes until all rotten, or -1.
follow_up_hints: Multi-source BFS from all initially rotten oranges simultaneously.

title: Walls and Gates
description: Fill each empty room with its distance to the nearest gate. INF = empty room, -1 = wall, 0 = gate.
follow_up_hints: Multi-source BFS from all gates simultaneously.

title: Course Schedule
description: Given numCourses and prerequisites, determine if you can finish all courses (detect cycle in directed graph).
follow_up_hints: Topological sort: if you can complete it, no cycle exists.

title: Course Schedule II
description: Return the order in which courses should be taken to finish all courses, or an empty array if impossible.
follow_up_hints: Kahn's algorithm: repeatedly remove nodes with in-degree 0.

title: Graph Valid Tree
description: Given n nodes (0 to n-1) and a list of undirected edges, determine if they form a valid tree.
follow_up_hints: Valid tree: exactly n-1 edges and connected. Use Union-Find.

title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
description: Given n nodes and a list of undirected edges, return the number of connected components.
follow_up_hints: Union-Find: count components after union all edges.

title: Redundant Connection
description: Given a graph that was a tree plus one extra edge, return the redundant edge.
follow_up_hints: Union-Find: the edge that joins two already-connected nodes is redundant.

title: Word Ladder
description: Given beginWord, endWord, and wordList, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence.
constraints: All words have the same length
All words consist of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: BFS; at each step try all one-letter substitutions and check against wordList set.

title: Reconstruct Itinerary
description: Given a list of airline tickets, reconstruct the itinerary in lexicographical order starting from JFK.
constraints: All airports consist of uppercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Hierholzer's algorithm for Eulerian path; sort adjacency lists for lexical order.

title: Min Cost to Connect All Points
description: Given n points, return the minimum cost to connect all points using Manhattan distance as edge weight (MST).
constraints: No two points are the same
follow_up_hints: Prim's algorithm: greedily add the closest unvisited point.

title: Network Delay Time
description: Given n nodes, edges with travel times, and source k, return the minimum time for all nodes to receive a signal from k.
follow_up_hints: Dijkstra from k; the answer is the maximum shortest distance.

title: Swim in Rising Water
description: In an n x n grid, swim from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1). At time t you can visit any cell with elevation <= t. Return the min t.
follow_up_hints: Dijkstra with cost = max elevation on path, or binary search + BFS.

title: Alien Dictionary
description: Given a sorted list of words from an alien language, determine the order of letters in the alphabet.
constraints: words[i] consists of lowercase English letters
Words are sorted lexicographically by alien language rules
follow_up_hints: Build directed graph from adjacent words; topological sort gives the order.

title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
description: Given n cities, flights with prices, find the cheapest price from src to dst with at most k stops.
follow_up_hints: Bellman-Ford for k+1 iterations; or BFS/Dijkstra with (cost, node, stops_left) state.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You can climb 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways can you climb n stairs?
follow_up_hints: dp[n] = dp[n-1] + dp[n-2] (Fibonacci pattern).

title: Min Cost Climbing Stairs
description: Given an array cost where cost[i] is the cost of step i, find the minimum cost to reach the top.
follow_up_hints: dp[i] = cost[i] + min(dp[i-1], dp[i-2]).

title: House Robber
description: Rob houses along a street; cannot rob two adjacent houses. Return the maximum amount you can rob.
follow_up_hints: dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i]).

title: House Robber II
description: Houses are arranged in a circle. Cannot rob adjacent houses. Return the maximum amount you can rob.
follow_up_hints: Run House Robber I twice: on nums[:-1] and nums[1:]; take the max.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring.
constraints: s consist of only digits and English letters
follow_up_hints: Expand around each center (odd and even lengths); track the longest.

title: Palindromic Substrings
description: Given a string s, return the number of palindromic substrings in it.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Expand around each center; count each valid palindrome.

title: Decode Ways
description: A string of digits can be decoded as A=1...Z=26. Return the number of ways to decode the message.
constraints: s does not contain leading zeros (except '0' itself)
follow_up_hints: dp[i] = ways to decode s[:i]; check 1-digit and 2-digit endings.

title: Coin Change
description: Given coins of different denominations and a total amount, return the fewest number of coins to make up that amount.
follow_up_hints: dp[i] = min coins to make amount i; initialize to infinity.

title: Maximum Product Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums, find the subarray with the largest product, and return the product.
constraints: The product of any subarray fits in a 32-bit integer
follow_up_hints: Track both current max and current min products (negatives can flip them).

title: Word Break
description: Given a string s and a dictionary wordDict, return true if s can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of dictionary words.
follow_up_hints: dp[i] = can s[:i] be segmented; try all words ending at position i.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
follow_up_hints: O(n^2) DP or O(n log n) with patience sorting (binary search on tails array).

title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
description: Given a non-empty array nums, determine if it can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum.
follow_up_hints: 0/1 knapsack: can we reach target = total_sum / 2?

title: Unique Paths
description: A robot starts at the top-left of an m x n grid and can only move right or down. Count distinct paths to the bottom-right.

title: Longest Common Subsequence
description: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence.
constraints: text1 and text2 consist of only lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: dp[i][j] = LCS of text1[:i] and text2[:j]; match or take best of skip.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
description: Maximize profit with a cooldown of one day after selling (cannot buy on the day immediately following a sell).
follow_up_hints: Three states: holding, just_sold (cooldown), idle. Transition between them each day.

title: Coin Change II
description: Given coin denominations and a total amount, return the number of combinations that make up the amount.
follow_up_hints: Unbounded knapsack: for each coin, update dp[j] += dp[j - coin].

title: Target Sum
description: Assign + or - to each element in nums so the sum equals target. Return the number of ways.
follow_up_hints: Reduce to subset sum: find subset P where 2*sum(P) = total + target.

title: Interleaving String
description: Determine if s3 is formed by interleaving s1 and s2.
follow_up_hints: dp[i][j] = can s3[:i+j] be formed from s1[:i] and s2[:j].

title: Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix
description: Given an m x n integer matrix, return the length of the longest increasing path.
follow_up_hints: DFS with memoization from each cell; move to strictly larger neighbors.

title: Distinct Subsequences
description: Given strings s and t, return the number of distinct subsequences of s that equal t.
constraints: s and t consist of English letters
follow_up_hints: dp[i][j] = ways to form t[:j] from s[:i]; if chars match, add dp[i-1][j-1].

title: Edit Distance
description: Given words word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations (insert, delete, replace) to convert word1 to word2.
constraints: word1 and word2 consist of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: dp[i][j] = edit distance between word1[:i] and word2[:j].

title: Burst Balloons
description: Burst all balloons to maximize coins. Bursting i gives nums[i-1]*nums[i]*nums[i+1].
follow_up_hints: Interval DP: dp[l][r] = max coins for balloons in (l, r); try each balloon as the last to burst.

title: Regular Expression Matching
description: Implement regular expression matching with '.' (any single char) and '*' (zero or more of preceding).
constraints: p contains only lowercase letters, '.', and '*'
follow_up_hints: dp[i][j] = does p[:j] match s[:i]; handle '*' by looking two positions back.

title: Maximum Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums, find the subarray with the largest sum and return its sum.
follow_up_hints: Kadane's algorithm: current = max(num, current + num); update global max.

title: Jump Game
description: Given an array where each element is the max jump length, determine if you can reach the last index.
follow_up_hints: Track the max reachable index; if current > max_reach, return false.

title: Jump Game II
description: Return the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index.
constraints: The test cases are generated such that you can reach nums[n-1]
follow_up_hints: Greedy: track current jump boundary; when you reach it, increment jumps.

title: Gas Station
description: There are n gas stations in a circle. Find the starting index from which you can complete the circuit.
follow_up_hints: If total gas >= total cost, a solution exists. Greedy: reset start when tank goes negative.

title: Hand of Straights
description: Determine if a hand of cards can be rearranged into groups of groupSize consecutive cards.
follow_up_hints: Count frequencies; greedily form groups starting from the smallest card.

title: Merge Triplets to Form Target Triplet
description: Given triplets [a,b,c] and a target, you can merge triplets (taking element-wise max). Return true if you can form the target.
follow_up_hints: Filter triplets where no element exceeds target; check if their OR equals target.

title: Partition Labels
description: Partition string s into as many parts as possible so each letter appears in at most one part. Return partition sizes.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters
follow_up_hints: Record last occurrence of each char; extend partition end greedily.

title: Valid Parenthesis String
description: Given a string with '(', ')', and '*' (which can be '(', ')', or empty), check if it's valid.
follow_up_hints: Track range [lo, hi] of possible open paren counts; hi < 0 means invalid.

title: Insert Interval
description: Given a sorted list of non-overlapping intervals and a new interval, insert it and merge if necessary.
follow_up_hints: Add all intervals ending before new, merge overlapping, add remaining.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals and return the result.
follow_up_hints: Sort by start; if current.start <= last.end, merge by extending last.end.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Find the minimum number of intervals to remove to make the rest non-overlapping.
follow_up_hints: Sort by end; greedily keep the interval with earliest end.

title: Meeting Rooms
description: Given an array of meeting time intervals, determine if a person can attend all meetings.
follow_up_hints: Sort by start; check if any interval starts before the previous one ends.

title: Meeting Rooms II
description: Find the minimum number of conference rooms required to hold all meetings.
follow_up_hints: Use a min-heap of end times; if earliest end <= current start, reuse the room.

title: Minimum Interval to Include Each Query
description: For each query value, find the smallest interval [l, r] where l <= query <= r. Return size r-l+1 or -1.
follow_up_hints: Sort intervals and queries; sweep with a min-heap of (size, end) for active intervals.

title: Rotate Image
description: Rotate an n x n matrix 90 degrees clockwise in-place.
follow_up_hints: Transpose the matrix, then reverse each row.

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements in spiral order.
follow_up_hints: Maintain top/bottom/left/right boundaries; shrink after each direction pass.

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
description: Given an m x n integer matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0.
follow_up_hints: Use first row and column as flag arrays to achieve O(1) space.

title: Happy Number
description: A happy number eventually reaches 1 when replaced by the sum of the square of its digits. Return true if n is happy.
follow_up_hints: Use a set to detect cycles, or Floyd's cycle detection.

title: Plus One
description: Given a large integer represented as a digit array, return the array of digits of the integer plus one.
constraints: digits does not contain leading zeros
follow_up_hints: Add 1 from the end; propagate carry as needed.

title: Pow(x, n)
description: Implement pow(x, n), which calculates x raised to the power n.
follow_up_hints: Fast exponentiation: square x and halve n at each step.

title: Multiply Strings
description: Given two non-negative integers represented as strings, return the product as a string.
constraints: num1 and num2 consist of digits only
Both num1 and num2 do not have leading zeros except the number '0' itself
follow_up_hints: Grade-school multiplication: digit i * digit j contributes to positions i+j and i+j+1.

title: Detect Squares
description: Given points added one by one, for each query point count how many axis-aligned squares can be formed using 3 points and the query.
constraints: At most 3000 calls to add and count
follow_up_hints: For each query, find pairs sharing x or y with query, check the diagonal for the 4th corner.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array where every element appears twice except for one, find that single one.
constraints: Every element appears twice except for exactly one
follow_up_hints: XOR all elements — pairs cancel out, leaving the single number.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Given a positive integer, return the number of set bits (Hamming weight).
follow_up_hints: n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit; count iterations until n = 0.

title: Counting Bits
description: Given n, return an array ans of length n+1 such that ans[i] = number of 1s in binary representation of i.
follow_up_hints: dp: bits[i] = bits[i >> 1] + (i & 1).

title: Reverse Bits
description: Reverse bits of a given 32-bit unsigned integer.
constraints: The input must be a binary string of length 32
follow_up_hints: Shift result left and OR in each bit from n, iterating 32 times.

title: Missing Number
description: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in [0, n], return the only number in the range missing.
follow_up_hints: XOR all indices and values, or use sum formula: n*(n+1)/2 - sum(nums).

title: Sum of Two Integers
description: Calculate the sum of two integers without using the + or - operators.
follow_up_hints: XOR gives sum without carry; AND << 1 gives the carry; repeat until no carry.

title: Reverse Integer
description: Given a signed 32-bit integer x, return x with its digits reversed. Return 0 if it overflows.
follow_up_hints: Build the reversed number digit by digit; check for 32-bit overflow before each step.
